blocks:
Surah Al-Mu'minun

Meccan, and it is 118 verses

(1) قَدْ أَفْلَحَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ (Certainly, the believers have attained salvation) In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: "Do you know who they are?" It was said: "You know best." He said: "Certainly, the believers have attained salvation (قَدْ أَفْلَحَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ), the Muslims (المسلمون). Indeed, the Muslims are the noble ones (النّجباء)." And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "When Allah created Paradise, He said to it: 'Speak.' So it said: 'Certainly, the believers have attained salvation (قَدْ أَفْلَحَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ).'"

(٢) الَّذِينَ هُمْ فِي صَلاتِهِمْ خاشِعُونَ ** (2) those who are humble in their prayers, Al-Qummi said: [It means] your lowering your gaze in your prayers and your turning towards them. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "When you enter into your prayers, you must observe humility (التخشّع) and turn towards your prayers, for Allah the Almighty says: الَّذِينَ هُمْ فِي صَلاتِهِمْ خاشِعُونَ." And narrated from him, from the Prophet (may the blessings of Allah be upon them both), he said: "Whatever increase there is in the humility (خشوع) of the body beyond what is in the heart, that is hypocrisy (نفاق) in Our view." In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: That He saw a man fiddling with his beard during his prayer, so He said: "Indeed, if his heart had been humble, his limbs (جوارحه) would have been humble." And it is narrated: That He used to raise His gaze to the sky during His prayer, but when the Ayah was revealed, He lowered His head and cast His gaze towards the ground.

(3) وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ عَنِ اللَّغْوِ مُعْرِضُونَ ** (3) and who avoid all indecent talk, Al-Qummi said: It means avoiding singing (الغناء) and amusements (الملاهي). And in Irshad al-Mufid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Every speech (قول) in which there is no remembrance (ذكر) is vain talk (لغو). And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: It is when a man speaks falsehood (بالباطل) against you or comes to you with something that is not in you, and you turn away from him for the sake of Allah. He said: And in another narration: It is singing (الغناء) and amusements (الملاهي). And in al-I'tiqadat, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about storytellers (القصاص): Is it permissible (يحل) to listen to them? He replied: No.

(٤) وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ لِلزَّكاةِ فاعِلُونَ (4) and those who are doers of zakat, al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever withholds a qirat (قيراط) of al-zakat (الزكاة) is neither a believer (مؤمن) nor a Muslim (مسلم), and there is no dignity (كرامة) for him." (٥) وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ لِفُرُوجِهِمْ حافِظُونَ (5) and those who guard their private parts, (٦) إِلَّا عَلى أَزْواجِهِمْ أَوْ ما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُهُمْ (6) except from their spouses or those whom their right hands own, al-Qummi said: meaning the female slaves (الإماء). He said: And the limit (حدّها) of al-mut'ah (المتعة) is the female slaves (الإماء). And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about al-mut'ah (المتعة), and He said: "It is lawful (حلال), so do not marry except a chaste woman (عفيفة). Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ لِفُرُوجِهِمْ حافِظُونَ (and those who guard their private parts)." And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): "Private parts (الفروج) become lawful (تحلّ) through three aspects (وجوه): marriage (نكاح) with inheritance (ميراث), marriage (نكاح) without inheritance (ميراث), and marriage (نكاح) through ownership of the right hand (ملك يمين)." And narrated from his father, from the Prophet (may the blessings of Allah be upon them both): "Indeed, Allah has made lawful (احلّ) for you private parts (الفروج) based on three meanings (معان): a private part that confers inheritance (مورّث), which is permanent marriage (الثبات); a private part that does not confer inheritance (غير مورّث), which is al-mut'ah (المتعة); and the ownership of your right hands (ملك ايمانكم). فَإِنَّهُمْ غَيْرُ مَلُومِينَ (they are not blameworthy)."

(٧) فَمَنِ ابْتَغى وَراءَ ذلِكَ (7) But whoever seeks [anything] beyond that— Al-Qummi said: Whoever exceeds that. فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ العادُونَ those—they are the transgressors. The complete ones (الكاملون) in transgression (العُدوان). (٨) وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ لِأَماناتِهِمْ (8) and those who are keepers of their trusts And it was recited (وقرئ) as لأمانتهم (for their trust, singular). وَعَهْدِهِمْ راعُونَ and covenants, [They are] watchful over what they are entrusted with and what they covenant, whether it be from the direction of the Truth (الحقّ) or the creation (الخلق), standing firm in preserving and rectifying them. (٩) وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ عَلى صَلاتِهِمْ (9) and those who are watchful of their prayers— And it was recited (وقرئ) as صَلَواتِهم (their prayers, plural). يُحافِظُونَ Al-Qummi said: Over their appointed times (أوقاتها) and their limits (حدودها). And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "It is the obligatory prayer (الفريضة)." It was said: [What about] الَّذِينَ هُمْ عَلى صَلاتِهِمْ دائِمُونَ (those who are constant at their prayers)? He said: "It is the supererogatory prayer (النّافلة)."

(١٠) أُولئِكَ هُمُ الْوارِثُونَ (Those are the inheritors) Those who combine these qualities are هُمُ الْوارِثُونَ (the inheritors). (١١) الَّذِينَ يَرِثُونَ الْفِرْدَوْسَ هُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ (who shall inherit Paradise, and will remain in it forever.)

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that he said: Allah did not create any creature except that He appointed for him a dwelling in Paradise and a dwelling in the Fire. When the people of Paradise dwell in Paradise and the people of the Fire dwell in the Fire, a caller will cry out: "O people of Paradise, look up!" So they will look up at the people of the Fire, and their dwellings therein will be raised for them. Then it will be said to them: "These are your dwellings that were in the Fire; had you disobeyed Allah, you would have entered them." He said: If anyone were to die of joy, the people of Paradise would die of joy on that day due to what was averted from them of the punishment. Then a caller will cry out: "O people of the Fire, raise your heads!" So they will raise their heads and look at their dwellings in Paradise and the bliss (النّعيم) contained therein. Then it will be said to them: "These are your dwellings that, had you obeyed your Lord, you would have entered." He said: If anyone were to die of grief, the people of the Fire would die of grief. Thus, these (the people of Paradise) inherit the dwellings of those (the people of the Fire), and those (the people of the Fire) inherit the dwellings of these (the people of Paradise). That is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: أُولئِكَ هُمُ الْوارِثُونَ الَّذِينَ يَرِثُونَ الْفِرْدَوْسَ هُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ. (Those are the inheritors, who shall inherit Paradise, and will remain in it forever.)

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), he said: There is not one among you except that he has two dwellings: a dwelling in Paradise and a dwelling in the Fire. If he dies and enters the Fire, the people of Paradise inherit his dwelling.

In al-Uyun, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), that he said: This verse is concerning me.

(١٢) وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا الْإِنْسانَ مِنْ سُلالَةٍ مِنْ طِينٍ. (12) Certainly We created man from an extract of clay.

Al-Qummi said: al-Sulālah (السلالة) is the pure essence (الصفوة) of food (الطعام) and drink (الشراب) which becomes a sperm-drop (نطفة). And the sperm-drop (النطفة) originates from al-Sulālah (السلالة), and al-Sulālah is from the pure essence (صفو) of food and drink, and the food (الطعام) originates from the essence (أصل) of clay (الطين). So this is the meaning of His saying, majestic is His remembrance: مِنْ سُلالَةٍ مِنْ طِينٍ. (from an extract of clay.)

(١٣) ثُمَّ جَعَلْناهُ نُطْفَةً فِي قَرارٍ مَكِينٍ ** (13) Then We made him a drop of [seminal] fluid in a secure abode. He said: meaning in the two testicles (الأنثيين) and then in the womb (الرّحم). (١٤) ثُمَّ خَلَقْنَا النُّطْفَةَ عَلَقَةً فَخَلَقْنَا الْعَلَقَةَ مُضْغَةً فَخَلَقْنَا الْمُضْغَةَ عِظاماً فَكَسَوْنَا الْعِظامَ لَحْماً ** (14) Then We created the drop of fluid as a clinging mass, and then We created the clinging mass as a lump of flesh, and then We created the lump of flesh as bones, and then We clothed the bones with flesh. Its interpretation has already preceded in the beginning of Surat al-Hajj. And (العظم) was read in the singular (التوحيد) in both instances. ثُمَّ أَنْشَأْناهُ خَلْقاً آخَرَ Then We produced him as a different creation. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that he said: It is the blowing of the Spirit (الرّوح) into him. فَتَبارَكَ اللهُ أَحْسَنُ الْخالِقِينَ So blessed is Allah, the best of creators! In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked whether anyone other than the Glorious Creator (الخالق الجليل) is a creator (خالق). He said: Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, said: فَتَبارَكَ اللهُ أَحْسَنُ الْخالِقِينَ ** (So blessed is Allah, the best of creators!). And He has informed that among His servants are creators (خالقين) and non-creators (غير خالقين). Among them is Jesus son of Mary, who created مِنَ الطِّينِ كَهَيْئَةِ الطَّيْرِ ** (out of clay the figure of a bird) by the permission of Allah, and the Samiri, who created لَهُمْ عِجْلاً جَسَداً لَهُ خُوارٌ (for them a calf—a mere body that lowed).

(15) ثُمَّ إِنَّكُمْ بَعْدَ ذلِكَ لَمَيِّتُونَ (15) Then after that you will surely die. (16) ثُمَّ إِنَّكُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ تُبْعَثُونَ (16) Then on the Day of Resurrection you will surely be resurrected. (17) وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنا فَوْقَكُمْ سَبْعَ طَرائِقَ ** (17) Certainly We created above you seven pathways, seven heavens (سموات). It is said He named them pathways (طرائق) because some of them are layered (طُورِقَ) above others, like the layering (مُطَارَقَة) of a sandal (النّعل), and everything above it is like it, and it is its path (طريقه). وَما كُنَّا عَنِ الْخَلْقِ غافِلِينَ and We have not been oblivious of the creation. (18) وَأَنْزَلْنا مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً بِقَدَرٍ فَأَسْكَنَّاهُ فِي الْأَرْضِ (18) And We sent down water from the sky in a measured amount, and lodged it in the earth, Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They are the rivers (الأنّهار), the springs (العيون), and the wells (الآبار). And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It means the water of al-ʿAqīq (العقيق). I say: By al-ʿAqīq (العقيق), He means the valley (الوادي). And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): Indeed, Allah Almighty sent down five rivers from Paradise: Sayhūn (سيحون), which is the river of India (الهند); Jayhūn (جيحون), which is the river of Balkh (بلخ); Dijlah (دجلة) and al-Furāt (الفرات), which are the two rivers of Iraq (العراق); and al-Nīl (النيل), which is the river of Egypt (مصر). Allah sent them down from a single spring (عين واحدة) and caused them to flow in the earth, and placed in them benefits for people in various types of their livelihoods (معايشهم). That is His saying: وَأَنْزَلْنا مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً بِقَدَرٍ ** (And We sent down water from the sky in a measured amount,) the Ayah. وَإِنَّا عَلى ذَهابٍ بِهِ ** (and We are certainly able to take it away.) by corruption (بالإفساد) or by ascension (التصعيد) or by deepening (التعميق) such that its extraction (استنباطه) becomes impossible لَقادِرُونَ ** (We are certainly able) just as We were able to send it down. It is said that the indefiniteness (تنكير) of "taking away" (ذهاب) hints at the multiplicity of its methods (طرقه) and serves as an emphasis in the threat (الإيعاد) concerning it.

(19) فَأَنْشَأْنا لَكُمْ بِهِ جَنَّاتٍ مِنْ نَخِيلٍ وَأَعْنابٍ لَكُمْ فِيها فَواكِهُ كَثِيرَةٌ ** (19) Then We produced for you therewith gardens of date-palms and grapes, wherein there are many fruits for you, which you enjoy as fruit (تَتَفَكَّهُونَ بِهَا). وَمِنْها تَأْكُلُونَ ** and of them you eat, as nourishment (تَغَذِّيًا). (20) وَشَجَرَةً تَخْرُجُ مِنْ طُورِ سَيْناءَ ** (20) and a tree that issues from Mount Sinai, And it was recited (وَقُرِئَ) with the kasrah on the sīn (السِّين). تَنْبُتُ بِالدُّهْنِ وَصِبْغٍ لِلْآكِلِينَ ** producing oil and a seasoning for those who eat. Meaning, it grows with the thing that combines being an oil (دُهْنًا) with which one anoints himself (يُدَّهَنُ بِهِ) and from which light is kindled (يُسْرَجُ مِنْهُ), and being a condiment (إِدَامًا) in which bread is dipped (يُصْبَغُ فِيهِ الْخُبْزُ), meaning, it is immersed in it for use as a condiment (لِلِائْتِدَامِ). And the word تَنْبُتُ was recited (وَقُرِئَ) from the verb أَنْبَتَ in the meaning of نَبَتَ.

Al-Qummi said: It is the olive tree (شَجَرَةُ الزَّيْتُونِ), and it is a similitude for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام. Thus, al-Ṭūr (الطُّور) is the mountain, and Sīnā’ (سَيْنَاء) is the tree.

In al-Majmaʿ, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, that He said: "Olive oil (الزَّيْتُ) is a blessed tree (شَجَرَةٌ مُبَارَكَةٌ), so use it as a condiment (فَأْتَدِمُوا بِهِ) and anoint yourselves with it (وَادَّهِنُوا)."

In al-Tahdhīb, narrated from al-Bāqir عليه السلام: It was in the will (وَصِيَّة) of the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام: "Take me out to al-Ẓahr (الظَّهْر). When your feet descend and a wind meets you, bury me, for that is the beginning of Ṭūr Sīnā’ (طُورِ سَيْنَاءَ)." And they did that.

And from al-Ṣādiq عليه السلام, who mentioned al-Gharī (الْغَرَّى). He said: "It is a piece of the mountain upon which Allah spoke to Moses with a direct speech (تَكْلِيمًا), and upon which He sanctified Jesus عليه السلام with a sanctification (تَقْدِيسًا), and upon which He took Abraham as a close friend (خَلِيلًا), and He took Muhammad as a beloved one (حَبِيبًا), and He made it a dwelling (مَسْكَنًا) for the Prophets عليهم السلام. By Allah, no one more noble than the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام dwelt there after his two pure fathers, Adam and Noah."

(٢١) وَإِنَّ لَكُمْ فِي الْأَنْعامِ لَعِبْرَةً ** (21) And indeed, there is a lesson for you in the cattle You take heed of their states (محالها). نُسْقِيكُمْ ** And it was read with the opening (فتح) of the nūn. مِمَّا فِي بُطُونِها ** namely, the milk. وَلَكُمْ فِيها مَنافِعُ كَثِيرَةٌ ** in their backs, their wool, and their hair. وَمِنْها تَأْكُلُونَ and you eat of them.

(22) وَعَلَيْها وَعَلَى الْفُلْكِ تُحْمَلُونَ (22) and on them and on the ships you are carried. In the land (البرّ) and the sea (البحر), for the camels (الإبل) are the ship (سفينة) of the land (البرّ). (23) وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا نُوحاً إِلى قَوْمِهِ فَقالَ يا قَوْمِ اعْبُدُوا اللهَ ما لَكُمْ مِنْ إِلهٍ غَيْرُهُ أَفَلا تَتَّقُونَ (23) Certainly We sent Noah to his people. He said, 'O my people, worship Allah! You have no god other than Him. Will you not be wary [of Him]?' And it was recited with al-jarr (بالجرّ). أَفَلا تَتَّقُونَ ** Meaning, do you not fear that He will remove His favors (نعمه) from you? (24) فَقالَ الْمَلَأُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ قَوْمِهِ ما هذا إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُكُمْ يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَتَفَضَّلَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَلَوْ شاءَ اللهُ لَأَنْزَلَ مَلائِكَةً ما سَمِعْنا بِهذا فِي آبائِنَا الْأَوَّلِينَ (24) But the elite of his people who were faithless said, 'This is nothing but a human like yourselves, who seeks to gain superiority over you. Had Allah wished, He would have sent down angels. We have not heard of such a thing among our forefathers.' فَقالَ الْمَلَأُ ** (But the elite) means the nobles (الأشراف). الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ قَوْمِهِ ** (of his people who were faithless) [said this] to their common folk (لعوامهم). يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَتَفَضَّلَ عَلَيْكُمْ ** (who seeks to gain superiority over you) means that he seeks precedence (الفضل) over you and seeks to rule you (ويسودكم). وَلَوْ شاءَ اللهُ ** (Had Allah wished) that He send a messenger (رسولاً), لَأَنْزَلَ مَلائِكَةً ** (He would have sent down angels), meaning messengers (رسلاً). ما سَمِعْنا بِهذا فِي آبائِنَا الْأَوَّلِينَ (We have not heard of such a thing among our forefathers) meaning, [we have not heard] of the monotheism (التّوحيد) to which he calls us.

(٢٥) إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا رَجُلٌ بِهِ جِنَّةٌ ** (25) He is just a man possessed by a jinn. Madness (جنون), and because of it, he says that. فَتَرَبَّصُوا بِهِ ** So bear (it) and wait حَتَّى حِينٍ ** until a time, perhaps he will recover from his madness. (٢٦) قالَ رَبِّ انْصُرْنِي بِما كَذَّبُونِ ** (26) He said, 'My Lord! Help me against their denial.' Against them, by destroying them بِما كَذَّبُونِ ** because of their denial of me. (٢٧) فَأَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْهِ أَنِ اصْنَعِ الْفُلْكَ بِأَعْيُنِنا ** (27) So We revealed to him: 'Build the ark before Our eyes and [in accordance with] Our revelation. By Our preservation, lest you be afflicted therein, or a corrupter (مفسد) corrupt it for you, and (by) Our revelation (وَوَحْيِنا), Our command (أمرنا), and Our teaching you how to build (it). فَإِذا جاءَ أَمْرُنا (Then, when Our command comes) for the descent of the punishment وَفارَ التَّنُّورُ (and the oven gushes forth). In al-Jawami', it is narrated: That it was said to Noah (peace be upon him): "When you see the water boiling over from the oven (التنّور), embark, you and those with you, upon the ship (السّفينة)." When the water gushed forth (نبع) from the oven (التنّور), his wife informed him, and he embarked. The complete story has already preceded in Surat Hud (peace be upon him). فَاسْلُكْ فِيها ** So enter into it. It is said: سلك فيه (he entered into it) and سلك غيره (he made others enter). مِنْ كُلٍّ زَوْجَيْنِ اثْنَيْنِ ** The male and the female. And كل (kull) was recited without tanwin. وَأَهْلَكَ إِلَّا مَنْ سَبَقَ عَلَيْهِ الْقَوْلُ مِنْهُمْ (and your family—except those of them against whom the word has already gone forth) regarding their destruction and their disbelief (كفره). وَلا تُخاطِبْنِي فِي الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا (and do not plead with Me for those who have done wrong) by supplicating for their deliverance (الإِنجاء), إِنَّهُمْ مُغْرَقُونَ ** for they shall be drowned, inevitably (لا محالة).

(٢٨) فَإِذَا اسْتَوَيْتَ أَنْتَ وَمَنْ مَعَكَ عَلَى الْفُلْكِ فَقُلِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي نَجَّانا مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ ** (28) When you and those who are with you are settled in the ark, say, 'All praise belongs to Allah, who delivered us from the wrongdoing lot.') Like His saying, the Almighty: فَقُطِعَ دابِرُ الْقَوْمِ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ.** (45) So the last remnant of the wrongdoing lot was cut off, and all praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds.)

(29) وَقُلْ رَبِّ أَنْزِلْنِي مُنْزَلاً ** (29) And say, 'My Lord, land me at a landing place And it was read with the fathah on the mīm and the kasrah on the zāy مُبارَكاً وَأَنْتَ خَيْرُ الْمُنْزِلِينَ blessed, and You are the best of those who land [people].' In al-Faqih: The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said to Ali عليه السلام: "O Ali, when you land at a place, then say: اللهُمَ أَنْزِلْنِي مُنْزَلاً مُبارَكاً وَأَنْتَ خَيْرُ الْمُنْزِلِينَ ** (O Allah, land me at a blessed landing place, and You are the best of those who land [people].), and its goodness will be granted to you, and its evil will be repelled."

(٣٠) **إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ وَإِنْ كُنَّا لَمُبْتَلِينَ ** (Indeed, there are signs in that, and We have been putting [them] to the test.) And We have been surely testing Our servants with these signs.

And in Nahj al-Balagha: Indeed, Allah has protected you from Him being unjust toward you, but He has not prevented you from Him testing you. And Glorious is He Who speaks, He has said: إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ وَإِنْ كُنَّا لَمُبْتَلِينَ. (Indeed, there are signs in that, and We have been putting [them] to the test.)

(٣١) ثُمَّ أَنْشَأْنا مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ قَرْناً آخَرِينَ ** (31) Then We raised up after them another generation. They are 'Ād and Thamūd. (٣٢) فَأَرْسَلْنا فِيهِمْ رَسُولاً مِنْهُمْ ** (32) And We sent to them an apostle from among themselves, He is Hūd and Ṣāliḥ أَنِ اعْبُدُوا اللهَ ما لَكُمْ مِنْ إِلهٍ غَيْرُهُ أَفَلا تَتَّقُونَ [who said,] 'Worship Allah! You have no god other than Him. Will you not be wary [of Him]?' (٣٣) وَقالَ الْمَلَأُ مِنْ قَوْمِهِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَكَذَّبُوا بِلِقاءِ الْآخِرَةِ وَأَتْرَفْناهُمْ ** (33) The elite of his people, who were faithless and denied the encounter of the Hereafter, and whom We had given luxury, and We bestowed favors upon them (وانعمناهم) فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ما هذا إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُكُمْ يَأْكُلُ مِمَّا تَأْكُلُونَ مِنْهُ وَيَشْرَبُ مِمَّا تَشْرَبُونَ.** in the life of the world, said, 'This is nothing but a human like yourselves. He eats of what you eat, and drinks of what you drink.'

(34) وَلَئِنْ أَطَعْتُمْ بَشَراً مِثْلَكُمْ ** in what he commands you with إِنَّكُمْ إِذاً لَخاسِرُونَ ** where you have humiliated yourselves. وَلَئِنْ أَطَعْتُمْ بَشَراً مِثْلَكُمْ إِنَّكُمْ إِذاً لَخاسِرُونَ (34) (34) And if you obey a human being like yourselves, then you will surely be losers.)

(35) أَيَعِدُكُمْ أَنَّكُمْ إِذا مِتُّمْ وَكُنْتُمْ تُراباً وَعِظاماً ** stripped of flesh and sinews, that you will be brought forth from the graves (الأجداث). أَيَعِدُكُمْ أَنَّكُمْ إِذا مِتُّمْ وَكُنْتُمْ تُراباً وَعِظاماً أَنَّكُمْ مُخْرَجُونَ** (35) (35) Does he promise you that when you are dead and become dust and bones, you will be brought forth?)

(36) هَيْهاتَ هَيْهاتَ ** distant لِما تُوعَدُونَ . The lām (اللام) is for clarification (البيان), just as in هَيْتَ لَكَ. هَيْهاتَ هَيْهاتَ لِما تُوعَدُونَ** (36) (36) Far-fetched, far-fetched is what you are promised!)

(37) إِنْ هِيَ إِلَّا حَياتُنَا الدُّنْيا ** Life is nothing but our life in this world نَمُوتُ وَنَحْيا ** some of us die and some are born وَما نَحْنُ بِمَبْعُوثِينَ ** after death. إِنْ هِيَ إِلَّا حَياتُنَا الدُّنْيا نَمُوتُ وَنَحْيا وَما نَحْنُ بِمَبْعُوثِينَ** (37) (37) There is nothing but our life in this world: we die and we live, and we will not be resurrected.)

(38) إِنْ هُوَ ** He is not إِلَّا رَجُلٌ افْتَرى عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً ** regarding what he claims about our sending, and what he promises us concerning the Resurrection وَما نَحْنُ لَهُ بِمُؤْمِنِينَ ** confirmers (بمصدّقين). إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا رَجُلٌ افْتَرى عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً وَما نَحْنُ لَهُ بِمُؤْمِنِينَ** (38) (38) He is only a man who has fabricated a lie against Allah, and we will not believe him.)

(39) قالَ رَبِّ انْصُرْنِي ** against them, and take vengeance for me upon them بِما كَذَّبُونِ ** because of their denial of me. قالَ رَبِّ انْصُرْنِي بِما كَذَّبُونِ (39) (39) He said, 'My Lord! Help me against their denial.')

(40) قالَ عَمَّا قَلِيلٍ لَيُصْبِحُنَّ نادِمِينَ ** over the denial when they see the punishment. قالَ عَمَّا قَلِيلٍ لَيُصْبِحُنَّ نادِمِينَ** (40) (40) Said He, 'Before long they will surely become regretful.')

(41) فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الصَّيْحَةُ بِالْحَقِ ** the Cry of Gabriel (صيحة جبرئيل); He cried out a terrifying cry against them, from which their hearts shattered, and they died. And in this is an indication that the generation (القرن) were the people of Salih فَجَعَلْناهُمْ غُثاءً. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): الغثاء is the dry, lifeless vegetation of the earth. It was said: He likened them in their destruction to the scum of the torrent (غثاء السّيل), which is debris (حميلة), like the saying of the Arabs: "The valley carried him away" (سار به الوادي) for one who perished. فَبُعْداً لِلْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ ** It allows for the possibility of being a statement of fact (الاخبار) or a supplication (الدّعاء). فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الصَّيْحَةُ بِالْحَقِ فَجَعَلْناهُمْ غُثاءً فَبُعْداً لِلْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ (41) (41) So the Cry seized them deservedly, and We made them like scum. So away with the wrongdoing lot!)

(٤٢) ثُمَّ أَنْشَأْنا مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ قُرُوناً آخَرِينَ ** (42) Then We produced other generations after them. meaning the people of Salih, Lot, Shu'ayb, and others. (٤٣) ما تَسْبِقُ مِنْ أُمَّةٍ أَجَلَها ** (43) No nation can advance its term, the time appointed for its destruction وَما يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ ** nor can they postpone it. the term (الأجل). (٤٤) ثُمَّ أَرْسَلْنا رُسُلَنا تَتْرا ** (44) Then We sent Our apostles successively. meaning following one another, one after the other, derived from al-witr (الوتر) which is the odd number (الفرد). It was also recited with tanwin (التنوين). كُلَّ ما جاءَ أُمَّةً رَسُولُها كَذَّبُوهُ فَأَتْبَعْنا بَعْضَهُمْ بَعْضاً ** Whenever an apostle came to a nation, they denied him. So We made them follow one another [to destruction], in destruction وَجَعَلْناهُمْ أَحادِيثَ ** and We made them subjects of tales. meaning nothing remained of them except stories (حكايات) by which examples are set فَبُعْداً لِقَوْمٍ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ So away with a people who do not have faith!

(٤٥) ثُمَّ أَرْسَلْنا مُوسى وَأَخاهُ هارُونَ بِآياتِنا ** (45) Then We sent Moses and his brother Aaron with Our signs بالآيات التسع وَسُلْطانٍ مُبِينٍ ** and a manifest authority. (with) the nine signs (الآيات التسع) and a clear argument (حجّة واضحة) compelling (ملزمة) to the opponent (للخصم). (٤٦) إِلى فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَائِهِ فَاسْتَكْبَرُوا ** (46) to Pharaoh and his elite. But they acted arrogantly, from faith (الإِيمان) and following (المتابعة) وَكانُوا قَوْماً عالِينَ ** and they were a tyrannical lot. arrogant ones (متكبّرين). (٤٧) فَقالُوا أَنُؤْمِنُ لِبَشَرَيْنِ مِثْلِنا وَقَوْمُهُما لَنا عابِدُونَ ** (47) They said, 'Shall we believe two humans like ourselves, while their people are our slaves?' meaning that the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل) are servants (خادمون) submissive (منقادون) to us. (٤٨) فَكَذَّبُوهُما فَكانُوا مِنَ الْمُهْلَكِينَ ** (48) So they impugned them, and thus became among the destroyed. by drowning (بالغرق). (٤٩) وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنا مُوسَى الْكِتابَ ** (49) Certainly We gave Moses the Book, the Torah (التوراة) لَعَلَّهُمْ ** so that they might be guided. perhaps the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل) يَهْتَدُونَ ** to the knowledge (المعارف) and the statutes (الأحكام). (٥٠) وَجَعَلْنَا ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ وَأُمَّهُ آيَةً ** (50) We made the son of Mary and his mother a sign, by her giving birth to Him without contact (من غير مسيس) وَآوَيْناهُما إِلى رَبْوَةٍ ذاتِ قَرارٍ وَمَعِينٍ ** and sheltered them on a knoll, a settled place with a spring. And it was recited with the opening (فتح) of the letter rā (الرّاء). And We made their refuge a high place (مكاناً مرتفعاً) possessing a settled, level ground (منبسطة) suitable for dwelling (للاستقرار) and cultivation (والزرع), and a spring (وَمَعِينٍ), which is pure water (ماء طاهر) flowing upon the surface of the earth. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: The knoll (الربوة) is Najaf of Kufa (نجف الكوفة), and the spring (والمعين) is the Euphrates (الفرات). And in al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): The knoll (الربوة) is the Hira (حيرة) of Kufa (الكوفة) and its cultivated land (سوادها), and the settled place (والقرار) is the Mosque of Kufa (مسجد الكوفة), and the spring (والمعين) is the Euphrates (الفرات).

(٥١) يا أَيُّهَا الرُّسُلُ كُلُوا مِنَ الطَّيِّباتِ وَاعْمَلُوا صالِحاً إِنِّي بِما تَعْمَلُونَ عَلِيمٌ. (51) O apostles! Eat of the good things, and act righteously. Indeed, I know well what you do.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: Indeed, Allah is good (طَيِّب) and accepts nothing but what is good (طَيِّب). And He commanded the believers with what He commanded the Messengers. So He said: يا أَيُّهَا الرُّسُلُ كُلُوا مِنَ الطَّيِّباتِ (O apostles! Eat of the good things) And He said: يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا كُلُوا مِنْ طَيِّباتِ ما رَزَقْناكُمْ. (O you who have faith! Eat of the good things that We have provided you.)

(٥٢) وَإِنَّ هذِهِ أُمَّتُكُمْ أُمَّةً واحِدَةً. (52) Indeed, this community of yours is one community, Al-Qummi said: upon one doctrine (مذهب واحد). And it was read wa inna (وان) with kasrah (كسر), and with fathah (فتح) and takhfif (تخفيف) (i.e., wa anna). وَأَنَا رَبُّكُمْ فَاتَّقُونِ (And I am your Lord, so be wary of Me) regarding the breaking of the staff (شقّ العصا) and the disagreement of the word (مخالفة الكلمة). (٥٣) فَتَقَطَّعُوا أَمْرَهُمْ بَيْنَهُمْ ** (53) But they fragmented their affair among themselves, So they became ruined and separated, and they made their religion into scattered religions (أدياناً متفرّقة). زُبُراً (pieces) means pieces (قطعاً), which is the plural of zubur (زبور) which means a faction (الفرقة). كُلُّ حِزْبٍ (Every faction) from those who formed factions (المتحزّبين) بِما لَدَيْهِمْ ** (with what they have) of the religion فَرِحُونَ ** (are rejoicing), being pleased (معجبون), believing (معتقدون) that they are upon the truth (الحق). Al-Qummi said: Everyone who chooses a religion for himself is rejoicing in it.

(٥٤) فَذَرْهُمْ فِي غَمْرَتِهِمْ ** (54) So leave them in their confusion In their ignorance. He likened it to water that submerges the stature. حَتَّى حِينٍ ** until a time, until they are killed or they die. (٥٥) أَيَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّما نُمِدُّهُمْ بِهِ ** (55) Do they reckon that what We provide them with What We give them and make it a support for them, مِنْ مالٍ وَبَنِينَ ** of wealth and children, (an explanation of what (ما)). (٥٦) نُسارِعُ لَهُمْ فِي الْخَيْراتِ ** (56) We are hastening for them [a share] in the good things? In what contains their good and their honor. بَلْ لا يَشْعُرُونَ ** Nay, they are not aware that this is a gradual enticement (استدراج).

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq, from his father, from his forefathers (peace be upon them). He said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Verily, Allah the Almighty says: My believing servant grieves when I restrict something of the world upon him, yet that is closer for him to Me. And he rejoices when the world is expanded for him, yet that is further for him from Me." Then He recited this Ayah, then He said: "Verily, that is a trial (فتنة) for them."

footnote (١) انشقاق العصا : تفرق الأمر. (1) انشقاق العصا (The splitting of the staff): The dispersal of the matter (تفرق الأمر).

(٥٧) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ هُمْ مِنْ خَشْيَةِ رَبِّهِمْ مُشْفِقُونَ ** (57) Indeed, those who are apprehensive for the fear of their Lord, from the fear of His punishment, being cautious. (٥٨) وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ بِآياتِ رَبِّهِمْ يُؤْمِنُونَ. (58) and those who believe in the signs of their Lord, (٥٩) وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ بِرَبِّهِمْ لا يُشْرِكُونَ ** (59) and those who do not associate partners with their Lord, neither an apparent (جليّاً) association nor a hidden (خفيّاً) one. (٦٠) وَالَّذِينَ يُؤْتُونَ ما آتَوْا ** (60) and those who give what they give, It was said: they give what they have given of charity (الصدقات). And al-Qummi said: [It refers to] worship (العبادة) and obedience (الطاعة). This is supported by the reading (قراءة) يأتون ما اتوا (they perform what they performed) found in the irregular readings (الشّواذ) and by what comes in the narrations (الروايات). وَقُلُوبُهُمْ وَجِلَةٌ ** (and their hearts are fearful), fearing that it may not be accepted from them, and that it may not occur in the proper manner (الوجه اللائق), leading to them being held accountable for it. **أَنَّهُمْ إِلى رَبِّهِمْ راجِعُونَ ** (because they will return to their Lord), because their return is to Him, and He knows what is hidden from them.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "From what is their apprehension (إشفاقهم) and their hope (رجائهم)? They fear that their deeds may be rejected if they do not obey Allah, the Mighty and Majestic is His mention, and they hope that [their deeds] will be accepted from them."

And in al-Majma', narrated from Him (peace be upon him): **وَقُلُوبُهُمْ وَجِلَةٌ ** (and their hearts are fearful) means fearing that [their deeds] may not be accepted from them. He said:

And in another narration: He performs what he performed while he is fearful and hopeful (خائف راج).

And in al-Mahasin, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): Regarding this Ayah, He said: "They perform the deeds they performed while they know that they will be rewarded for them."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: "If you are able not to be known, then do so. What harm is it to you if people do not praise you? And what harm is it to you to be blamed by people if you are praised by Allah?" Then He said: My father said: 'Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) said: "There is no good in life except for two types of men: a man who increases in goodness every day, and a man who rectifies an evil deed with repentance (التوبة)." "And how can he achieve repentance (التوبة)? By Allah, even if he were to prostrate until his neck breaks, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, would not accept it from Him except through our Wilayah (بولايتنا), the People of the House (أهل البيت)." "Indeed, whoever recognizes our right (حقّنا), hopes for the reward through us, and is content with his sustenance being half a mudd (نصف مدّ) every day, and what covers his nakedness, and what shelters his head—by Allah, they are, in that state, fearful (خائفون) and apprehensive (وجلون). They would wish that this were their entire share of the world." "And thus did Allah the Almighty describe them, saying: وَالَّذِينَ يُؤْتُونَ ما آتَوْا وَقُلُوبُهُمْ وَجِلَةٌ أَنَّهُمْ إِلى رَبِّهِمْ راجِعُونَ ** (and those who give what they give, and their hearts are fearful, because they will return to their Lord)." Then He said: "What is it that they performed (أتوا)? By Allah, they performed obedience (الطاعة) along with love (المحبة) and Wilayah (الولاية), and yet they are fearful in that state. Their fear is not the fear of doubt (شك), but rather they feared that they might have been negligent (مقصّرين) in their love (محبّتنا) and obedience (طاعتنا) to us."

(٦١) أُولئِكَ يُسارِعُونَ فِي الْخَيْراتِ ** (61) Those are the ones who hasten to good works, They desire acts of obedience (الطاعات) with the strongest desire, so they rush to them. وَهُمْ لَها سابِقُونَ (and they are the foremost in them.) In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He is Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him); no one preceded him.

(٦٢) وَلا نُكَلِّفُ نَفْساً إِلَّا وُسْعَها ** (62) We task no soul except according to its capacity, less than its full capability (طاقتها). He intends by this to encourage [people] toward what He described the righteous ones (الصالحون) with, and to make it easy upon the souls. وَلَدَيْنا كِتابٌ [The Book] is the scroll of deeds (صحيفة الأعمال) يَنْطِقُ بِالْحَقِ [It speaks] with truth (بالصدق); nothing is found in it that contradicts reality. وَهُمْ لا يُظْلَمُونَ ** and they will not be wronged. by an increase in punishment (عقاب) or a decrease in reward (ثواب).

In al-Manaqib, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): When the month of Ramadan began, He (peace be upon him) would write down the sins of His servants (غلمانه). Then, when it was the last night, He would call them, display the book, and say: "O so-and-so, you did such-and-such, and I did not discipline you." They would all confess. Then He would stand in their midst and say: "Raise your voices and say: 'O Ali ibn al-Husayn, your Lord has enumerated what you have done, just as you have enumerated [what we have done] against us. And with Him is كِتابٌ يَنْطِقُ بِالْحَقِ لا يُغادِرُ صَغِيرَةً وَلا كَبِيرَةً إِلَّا أَحْصاها ** (a Book that speaks the truth, leaving out nothing, big or small, but that He has enumerated it.)'" "So remember the humiliation of your standing before your Lord, Who does not wrong [anyone] by the weight of an atom. وَكَفى بِاللهِ شَهِيداً ** (and Allah suffices as witness.) Therefore, pardon and overlook, and the Sovereign (المليك) will pardon you, due to His Almighty saying: **وَلْيَعْفُوا وَلْيَصْفَحُوا أَلا تُحِبُّونَ أَنْ يَغْفِرَ اللهُ لَكُمْ ** (And let them pardon and forbear. Do you not desire that Allah should forgive you?)" And He (peace be upon him) would weep and lament.

(٦٣) بَلْ قُلُوبُهُمْ The hearts of the disbelievers (الكفرة) فِي غَمْرَةٍ in a stupor (غفلة) that overwhelms them مِنْ هذا It was said: [It refers to] that which these people were described with, or from the book of the guardian angels (الحفظة). And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) means: from the Quran. وَلَهُمْ أَعْمالٌ evil (خبيثة) مِنْ دُونِ ذلِكَ other than what they are upon, which is polytheism (الشرك) هُمْ لَها عامِلُونَ They are accustomed to doing them.

بَلْ قُلُوبُهُمْ فِي غَمْرَةٍ مِّنْ هَذَا وَلَهُمْ أَعْمَالٌ مِّن دُونِ ذَلِكَ هُمْ لَهَا عَامِلُونَ ٦٣ (63) Nay, their hearts are in a stupor concerning this, and they have other deeds besides that which they are doing.

(٦٤) حَتَّى إِذا أَخَذْنا مُتْرَفِيهِمْ ** (64) When We seize their affluent ones their pampered ones (متنعّميهم). Al-Qummi says: meaning their leaders (كبرائهم) بِالْعَذابِ (with the punishment). In al-Jawami': It is their killing on the Day of Badr, or the hunger when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم prayed against them, saying: "O Allah, intensify Your grip (وطأتك) upon Mudar and make it years upon them like the years of Yusuf (peace be upon him)." So He afflicted them with drought (القحط) until they ate the carcasses (الجيف), dogs (الكلاب), burnt bones (العظام المحترقة), filth (القذر), and children (الأولاد). إِذا هُمْ يَجْأَرُونَ ** behold, they cry out [for help]. they were suddenly struck by screaming (الصّراخ) with pleas for help (بالاستغاثة).

(٦٥) لا تَجْأَرُوا الْيَوْمَ إِنَّكُمْ مِنَّا لا تُنْصَرُونَ ** (65) 'Do not cry out today! Indeed, you will not be helped by Us.' That was said to them. (٦٦) قَدْ كانَتْ آياتِي تُتْلى عَلَيْكُمْ فَكُنْتُمْ عَلى أَعْقابِكُمْ تَنْكِصُونَ ** (66) 'My signs used to be recited to you, but you would turn back on your heels,' You would turn away, retreating from hearing them, believing in them, and acting upon them. And al-nukūs (النكوص) is retreating backward (الرجوع قهقرى). (٦٧) مُسْتَكْبِرِينَ بِهِ ** (67) 'being disdainful of it, talking nonsense about it in your night talks.' It was said: meaning, by the Quran (بالقرآن), by including the meaning of denial (التكذيب) within al-istikbār (الاستكبار). And it was said: meaning, by the Ancient House (بالبيت العتيق), and the notoriety of their arrogance (استكبارهم) and their boasting (افتخارهم) that they were its custodians (قوامه) made it unnecessary to mention it previously. سامِراً (sāmiran): meaning, they would hold night talks (يسمرون) mentioning the Quran and slandering it (الطّعن فيه). It was said that they would tell stories (يقصّون) at night in their gatherings around the House (حول البيت). تَهْجُرُونَ ** (tahjurūn): Either from al-hajr (الهجر) meaning abandonment (القطيعة) or delirium (الهذيان); meaning, you turn away from the Quran (تعرضون من القرآن) or you speak deliriously (تهذون) concerning its matter. Or, it is from al-hujr (الهجر) (with a ḍamma on the hā) meaning obscenity (الفحش). And it was recited with a ḍamma on the tā' (بضم التاء).

(٦٨) أَفَلَمْ يَدَّبَّرُوا الْقَوْلَ ** (68) (Do they not contemplate the Word) meaning the Quran, so that they may know that it is the truth from their Lord, due to the miraculous nature (إعجاز) of its wording (لفظه) and the clarity (وضوح) of its meaning (مدلوله). أَمْ جاءَهُمْ ما لَمْ يَأْتِ آباءَهُمُ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (Or has there come to them what did not come to their forefathers of old?) namely, the Messenger (الرسول) and the Book (الكتاب). And in al-Jawami': [This refers to a time] when they feared Allah, so they believed in Him and obeyed Him. He said: And their forefathers (آباؤهم) are Isma'il (إسماعيل) and his descendants (أعقابه). And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Do not revile (تسبّوا) Mudar (مضر) nor Rabi'ah (ربيعة), for both of them were Muslims. And do not revile al-Harith ibn Ka'b (الحارث بن كعب), nor Asad ibn Khuzaymah (أسد بن خزيمة), nor Tamim ibn Murrah (تميم بن مرّة), for they were upon Islam (الإِسلام). And whatever you doubt concerning anything, do not doubt that Tubba' (تبعاً) was a Muslim."

(٦٩) أَمْ لَمْ يَعْرِفُوا رَسُولَهُمْ ** (69) Have they not recognized their Apostle, by trustworthiness (بالامانة), truthfulness (والصدق), good character (وحسن الخلق), and perfection of knowledge (وكمال العلم) despite not having been taught (مع عدم التعلم), and other such qualities which are characteristics (صفة) of the Prophets فَهُمْ لَهُ مُنْكِرُونَ so they deny him? (٧٠) أَمْ يَقُولُونَ بِهِ جِنَّةٌ ** (70) Or do they say, 'There is a madness in him'? so they do not care about his saying, even though they knew that he was the soundest of them in intellect (أرجحهم عقلاً) and the firmest of them in judgment (واثبتهم نظراً). بَلْ جاءَهُمْ بِالْحَقِّ وَأَكْثَرُهُمْ لِلْحَقِّ كارِهُونَ ** Rather, He has brought them the truth, but most of them are averse to the truth. because it contradicts their desires (شهواتهم) and their caprices (أهواءهم), and for that reason they denied it. It was said that the ruling was restricted to "most" (بالأكثر) only because some among them abandoned faith (الإِيمان) out of disdain (استنكافاً) for the reproach of their people, or due to their lack of insight (قلّة فطنته) and absence of reflection (عدم فكرته), not because of aversion to the truth (كراهة الحق).

(٧١) وَلَوِ اتَّبَعَ الْحَقُّ أَهْواءَهُمْ لَفَسَدَتِ السَّماواتُ وَالْأَرْضُ وَمَنْ فِيهِنَ ** (71) And if the truth were to follow their desires, the heavens and the earth and whoever is in them would surely be corrupted. That by which the universe subsists would vanish, and nothing would remain. al-Qummi said: الْحَقُ ** (the truth) refers to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام. He said: The corruption of the heaven is when it does not rain, and the corruption of the earth is when it does not grow vegetation, and the corruption of the people is in that.

بَلْ أَتَيْناهُمْ بِذِكْرِهِمْ (Rather, We have brought them their reminder)

(We brought them) the Book which is their reminder (ذِكْرَهُمْ), meaning their admonition (وَعْظَهُمْ), their testament (أَوْصِيَتَهُمْ), and their glory (فَخْرَهُمْ); or (it refers to) the reminder (الذِّكْر) which they wished for when they said:

لَوْ أَنَّ عِنْدَنا ذِكْراً مِنَ الْأَوَّلِينَ فَهُمْ عَنْ ذِكْرِهِمْ مُعْرِضُونَ (If only we had a reminder from the ancients!) yet they are disregardful of their own reminder.

(٧٢) أَمْ تَسْأَلُهُمْ خَرْجاً ** (72) Or do you ask them for a tribute, a wage (أجراً) for conveying the message (الرسالة). فَخَراجُ رَبِّكَ خَيْرٌ ** for the tribute of your Lord is better, So His wage (أجره) in this world and the Hereafter is better, for in it there is goodness due to its vastness (لسعته) or its permanence (دوامه). Thus, in it there is sufficiency (مندوحة) for you, dispensing with their giving. And الخرج (tribute/outlay) is in opposition to الدخل (income/inlay), and الخراج (land tax/revenue) is generally used for the tax (الضريبة) on the land. Thus, in it there is an indication (اشعار) of abundance (الكثرة) and necessity (اللّزوم). And الخرج was recited in both places, and الخراج was recited in both of them. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: أَمْ تَسْأَلُهُمْ خَرْجاً فَخَراجُ رَبِّكَ خَيْرٌ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ الرَّازِقِينَ ** Or do you ask them for a tribute, for the tribute of your Lord is better, and He is the best of providers. This is an affirmation (تقرير) of the goodness (خيريّة) of His tribute (خراجه).

(٧٣) وَإِنَّكَ لَتَدْعُوهُمْ إِلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ (73) And indeed you invite them to a straight path. Al-Qummi said: [It refers] to the guardianship (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). (٧٤) وَإِنَّ الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ عَنِ الصِّراطِ لَناكِبُونَ ** (74) And indeed those who do not believe in the Hereafter are swerving from the path. Meaning, deviating (عادلون) from it, for the fear of the Hereafter is the strongest motivator (البواعث) for seeking the truth (الحق) and traversing its way (طريقه). Al-Qummi said, narrated from the Imam (الإِمام): "They are certainly deviating (لحادّون)." And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "Indeed, if Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, had willed, He would have introduced Himself to the servants, but He made us His gates (أبوابه), His path (صراطه), His way (سبيله), and the direction (الوجه) through which He is approached. So whoever deviates (عدل) from our guardianship (ولايتنا) and prefers others over us, then indeed they عَنِ الصِّراطِ لَناكِبُونَ** (are swerving from the path)."

(٧٥) وَلَوْ رَحِمْناهُمْ وَكَشَفْنا ما بِهِمْ مِنْ ضُرٍّ ** (75) Had We mercy on them and removed the distress that they have meaning the drought (القحط). لَلَجُّوا ** they would persist (لَتَمَادَوْا) فِي طُغْيانِهِمْ ** in their excess (إِفْرَاطِهِمْ) in disbelief (الْكُفْر), arrogance (الِاسْتِكْبَار) regarding the truth (الْحَقّ), and enmity toward the Messenger (الرَّسُول) and the believers (الْمُؤْمِنِينَ). يَعْمَهُونَ ** from guidance (الْهُدَى).

It is narrated: that they suffered drought (قَحَطُوا) until they ate al-ʿilhiz (الْعِلْهِز). Then Abu Sufyan came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "I implore you by Allah and by kinship (الرَّحِم). Do you not claim that you were sent as a mercy to the worlds (رَحْمَةً لِلْعَالَمِينَ)? You have killed the fathers by the sword and the sons by hunger." So [this verse] was revealed. Thus it is mentioned in al-Jawamiʿ (الْجَوَامِع).

(٧٦) وَلَقَدْ أَخَذْناهُمْ بِالْعَذابِ ** (76) Certainly We seized them with the punishment, It was said that this means the killing on the Day of Badr. And according to al-Qummi, it is hunger, fear, and killing. فَمَا اسْتَكانُوا لِرَبِّهِمْ وَما يَتَضَرَّعُونَ ** but they did not humble themselves before their Lord, nor did they entreat [Him]. Rather, they persisted in their insolence (عُتُوّ) and arrogance (اِسْتِكْبَار).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah. He said: "The humbling (الاستكانة) is submission (الخضوع), and the entreating (التضرّع) is raising the hands and supplicating (التضرع) with them."

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The humbling (الاستكانة) is supplication (الدعاء), and the entreating (التضرع) is raising the hands during the prayer (الصلاة).

(77) حَتَّى إِذا فَتَحْنا عَلَيْهِمْ باباً ذا عَذابٍ شَدِيدٍ (until, when We open for them a door to a severe punishment) In al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): That was when the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم prayed, saying: "O Allah, make it years upon them like the years of Joseph (peace be upon him)." So they starved until they ate (العلهز) (1), which is wool mixed with blood. And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It is concerning the Return (الرجعة). إِذا هُمْ فِيهِ مُبْلِسُونَ ** (when behold, they are dumbfounded in it)—bewildered (متحيّرون), despairing (آيسون) of all good, until the richest among them came to you seeking your compassion.

(1) (العلهز): with a kasrah, means the large tick (القراد الضخم), and a food made of blood and wool (الوبر) that was prepared during famine.

(٧٨) وَهُوَ الَّذِي أَنْشَأَ لَكُمُ السَّمْعَ وَالْأَبْصارَ ** (78) And it is He who produced for you the hearing and the sight so that you may perceive with them what has been established of the signs (الآيات), وَالْأَفْئِدَةَ ** (and the hearts/intellects) so that you may reflect upon them and use them as evidence for that which is beyond that of benefits. قَلِيلاً ما تَشْكُرُونَ ** (Little do you thank) You thank them with little thanks, because the foundation (الْعُمْدَة) of thanking for them is using them in what they were created for, and submitting (الْإِذْعَان) to the One Who bestowed them without associating partners (إِشْرَاك).

(٧٩) وَهُوَ الَّذِي ذَرَأَكُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ (79) It is He who created you on the earth, He created you and spread you therein through procreation. وَإِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ and toward Him you will be mustered. you will be gathered after your dispersal.

(٨٠) وَهُوَ الَّذِي يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ وَلَهُ اخْتِلافُ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهارِ أَفَلا تَعْقِلُونَ (80) And it is He who gives life and brings death, and the alternation of the night and the day belongs to Him. Do you not exercise your reason? by means of reflection (بالنظر) and contemplation (والتأمّل), that all is from Us (منّا) and that Our power encompasses every single thing.
(81) Rather, they said—the disbelievers of Mecca—مِثْلَ ما قالَ الْأَوَّلُونَ (like what the former people said)

(٨٢) قالُوا أَإِذا مِتْنا وَكُنَّا تُراباً وَعِظاماً أَإِنَّا لَمَبْعُوثُونَ (82) They said, 'When we are dead and become dust and bones, shall we indeed be resurrected?' Expressing improbability (استبعاداً). And they did not reflect that they were also dust before that, and then they were created.

(٨٣) لَقَدْ وُعِدْنا نَحْنُ وَآباؤُنا هذا مِنْ قَبْلُ إِنْ هذا إِلَّا أَساطِيرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ (83) We and our fathers were promised this before. This is nothing but myths of the ancients.

meaning their lies (أكاذيبهم) which they wrote. It is the plural of ustūr (اسطور), because it is used for that which is entertained by, such as wonders (الأعاجيب) and jokes (الأضاحيك). And it is said that it is the plural of asṭār (اسطار), which is the plural of saṭr (سطر).
(٨٤) قُلْ لِمَنِ الْأَرْضُ وَمَنْ فِيها إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ. (84) Say, ‘To whom belongs the earth and whoever is in it, if you know?’

(٨٥) سَيَقُولُونَ لِلَّهِ (85) They will say, 'To Allah.' because sound reason (العقل الصريح) compelled them, with the slightest consideration (بأدنى نظر), [to acknowledge] that He is their Creator. قُلْ (Say) after they said it, أَفَلا تَذَكَّرُونَ (Will you not take admonition?) so that you may know that the One Who originated (فطر) the earth and those within it initially (ابتداءً) is capable of bringing them into existence a second time (ثانياً), and that the beginning of creation (بدء الخلق) is not easier than its repetition (إعادته).
(٨٦) قُلْ مَنْ رَبُّ السَّماواتِ السَّبْعِ وَرَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ (86) Say, 'Who is the Lord of the seven heavens, and the Lord of the Great Throne?' For indeed, they are greater than that.

(٨٧) سَيَقُولُونَ لِلَّهِ And it was recited without the لَام (preposition) in this (verse) and in what follows it, according to what the wording of the question necessitates. قُلْ أَفَلا تَتَّقُونَ (87) They will say, 'They belong to Allah.' Say, 'Will you not then be wary [of Him]?' (Be wary of) His punishment (عقابه), so do not associate some of His creatures with Him, and do not deny His power over some of His capabilities (مقدوراته).

(٨٨) قُلْ مَنْ بِيَدِهِ مَلَكُوتُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ (88) Say, 'Who is it in whose hand is the dominion of all things, The King Who is entrusted with it. وَهُوَ يُجِيرُ ** while He protects and none can protect against Him, He grants succor to whom He wills and protects him. وَلا يُجارُ عَلَيْهِ ** while He protects and none can protect against Him, And no one is granted succor nor is he protected. And its transitive usage with (عَلَى) is due to the inclusion of the meaning of aid (النصرة). إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ if you know?'

(٨٩) سَيَقُولُونَ لِلَّهِ قُلْ فَأَنَّى تُسْحَرُونَ (89) They will say, 'It belongs to Allah.' Say, 'Then how are you being bewitched?' So from where are you being deceived, that you are turned away from the right guidance (الرشد), despite the clarity of the matter (الأمر) and the manifestation of the proofs (الأدلّة)?

(90) بَلْ أَتَيْناهُمْ بِالْحَقِ (Nay, We brought them the truth) from monotheism (التوحيد) and the promise of resurrection (النشور). وَإِنَّهُمْ لَكاذِبُونَ (And indeed they are liars) where they denied that.

(٩١) مَا اتَّخَذَ اللهُ مِنْ وَلَدٍ due to His sanctity from resembling anyone. وَما كانَ مَعَهُ مِنْ إِلهٍ (And there is no god along with Him) who shares with Him in divinity (الألوهيّة). إِذاً لَذَهَبَ كُلُّ إِلهٍ بِما خَلَقَ (for then each god would take away what he created) and monopolize it, and his dominion would be distinct from the dominion of another. وَلَعَلا بَعْضُهُمْ عَلى بَعْضٍ (and some of them would surely dominate others.) as is the state of the kings of the world. Thus, this flawless management (التدبير المحكم), its connection, and the reliance of some parts upon others, indicate a single Maker (صانع واحد). سُبْحانَ اللهِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ (Clear is Allah of what they attribute [to Him]!) such as offspring and partner.

(٩٢) عالِمِ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهادَةِ (Knower of the unseen and the visible) فَتَعالى عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ (So exalted is He above what they ascribe as partners [to Him]!) And it was recited with al-raf' (الرفع).

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Al-Ghayb (الغيب) is that which has not yet come to be, and al-Shahādah (الشهادة) is that which has already come to be.

(٩٣) قُلْ رَبِّ إِمَّا تُرِيَنِّي (93) Say, 'My Lord, if You should show me—' If it is inevitable that You show me, then the مَا and the نُون are for emphasis (لِلتَّأْكِيد). ما يُوعَدُونَ (93) what they are promised,'

رَبِّ فَلا تَجْعَلْنِي فِي الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ (94) (My Lord! Do not put me among the wrongdoing lot!) (meaning) a companion for them.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, who said during the Farewell Pilgrimage while He was in Mina: "Do not return after me as disbelievers (كفّار) where some of you strike the necks of others. By Allah, if you do this, you will surely recognize me in a battalion fighting you." The narrator said: Then someone behind him grasped his left shoulder, so He turned and said: "Or 'Ali?" Then [the verse] was revealed.

I say: And from the way of the Khassah (الخاصّة) is what Sa'd ibn 'Abd Allah narrated in his Mukhtasar Basa'ir with his chain of transmission from Jabir ibn 'Abd Allah. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said, having addressed us on the Day of the Conquest: "O people, I will surely know if you return after me as disbelievers (كفّار), where some of you strike the necks of others. And if you do this, I will strike you with the sword." Then He turned to His right, and the people said that Gabriel (جبرئيل) nudged Him, so He said to him: "Or 'Ali? Or 'Ali?"

And in the narration of Aban ibn Taghlib from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, he said: Then Gabriel (جبرئيل) descended upon Him and said: "O Muhammad, if Allah wills, or that will be 'Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام." The Messenger of Allah said: "Or that will be 'Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام." Then Gabriel (جبرئيل) said to Him: "One is for you, and two are for 'Ali عليه السلام, and your appointed time is al-Salam (السلام)." Aban said: "May I be made your sacrifice, and where is al-Salam (السلام)?" He replied: "O Aban, al-Salam (السلام) is from the outskirts of Kufa."

I say: And that will only occur during the Return (الرجعة).
(٩٥) وَإِنَّا عَلى أَنْ نُرِيَكَ ما نَعِدُهُمْ لَقادِرُونَ (95) and We are indeed able to show you what We promise them. It means الرجعة (the Return).

(٩٦) ادْفَعْ بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ (96) Repel [evil] with what is best.

[Repel] السَّيِّئَةَ (the evil deed) by overlooking it (الصّفح عنها) and acting kindly (الإحسان) in return for it. And this is more eloquent than [repelling] the evil deed with a good deed (بالحسنة السيئة), because in it there is explicit mention of the detail (التنصيص على التفصيل).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ ** (with what is best), [it means] التقيّة (precautionary dissimulation).

**نَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِما يَصِفُونَ ** (We are more knowing of what they describe) [means] with what they describe you with.

** (٩٧) وَقُلْ رَبِّ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ هَمَزاتِ الشَّياطِينِ (And say, 'My Lord! I seek refuge in You from the promptings of the devils) their whisperings (وساوسهم). And the origin of al-hamz (الهمز) is the prick (النّخس). Al-Qummi said: What falls into your heart comes from the whispering (وسوسة) of the devils (الشّياطين).
(98) And I seek refuge in You, my Lord, from them attending me and from them hovering around me in any of the circumstances.

(٩٩) حَتَّى إِذا جاءَ أَحَدَهُمُ الْمَوْتُ (99) Until, when death comes to one of them [It is] related to (يَصِفُونَ) [they describe], and what is between them is a parenthetical clause (اعتراض). He says, regretting what he neglected of faith (الايمان) and obedience (الطاعة), when he becomes aware of the reality (الأمر): رَبِّ ارْجِعُونِ ** My Lord! Send me back! Return me to the world (الدنيا). And the wāw is for magnifying the addressee (المخاطب), like His saying: "So have mercy on me, O God of Muhammad, for if I am not worthy (أهل), then You are worthy (أهل) of it."

(١٠٠) لَعَلِّي أَعْمَلُ صالِحاً فِيما تَرَكْتُ (100) That I may act righteously in what I have left behind.

Al-Qummi (says): It was revealed concerning the one who withholds al-Zakah (الزكاة).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever withholds al-Zakah (الزكاة) will ask for return (الرّجعة) at the time of death, and that is His saying, the Almighty: رَبِّ ارْجِعُونِ (100) لَعَلِّي أَعْمَلُ صالِحاً فِيما تَرَكْتُ (100) 'My Lord! Send me back, that I may act righteously in what I have left behind.' كَلَّا (100) 'No indeed!' (This is) a deterrent against seeking the return (الرّجعة) and deeming it far-fetched. إِنَّها كَلِمَةٌ هُوَ قائِلُها (100) It is but a word that he utters. due to the dominance of regret (الحسرة) over him. وَمِنْ وَرائِهِمْ (100) And ahead of them (meaning) before them (امامهم) بَرْزَخٌ إِلى يَوْمِ يُبْعَثُونَ. (100) is a barrier until the day they are resurrected.

Al-Qummi (says): Al-Barzakh (البرزخ) is a matter between two matters (أمرين), which is the reward (الثواب) and the punishment (العقاب) between this world (الدنيا) and the Hereafter (الآخرة). And this is the saying of al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "By Allah, I do not fear for you except al-Barzakh (البرزخ). As for when the matter returns to us, then we are more deserving of you."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was said to him: "I heard you saying that all of our followers (شيعتنا) are in Paradise (الجنّة), regardless of what they have done." He said: "I spoke the truth to you; all of them, by Allah, are in Paradise (الجنّة)." It was said: "But the sins (الذنوب) are many and great." He said: "As for the Day of Resurrection (القيامة), all of you will be in Paradise (الجنّة) through the intercession (شفاعة) of the obeyed Prophet or the successor (وصيّ) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. But I, by Allah, fear for you concerning al-Barzakh (البرزخ)." It was asked: "And what is al-Barzakh (البرزخ)?" He replied: "The grave (القبر), from the moment of his death until the Day of Resurrection (القيامة)."

And in al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): He recited this Ayah (الآية) and said: "It is the grave (القبر), and indeed, they will have a wretched life (معيشة ضنكا) therein. By Allah, the grave (القبر) is either a garden from the gardens of Paradise (الجنّة) or a pit from the pits of the Fire (النار)."

(١٠١) فَإِذا نُفِخَ فِي الصُّورِ لقيام الساعة فَلا أَنْسابَ بَيْنَهُمْ تنفعهم بالتعاطف والتراحم أو يفتخرون بها وذلك من فرط الحيرة واستيلاء الدّهشة بحيث يَفِرُّ الْمَرْءُ مِنْ أَخِيهِ وَأُمِّهِ وَأَبِيهِ وَصاحِبَتِهِ وَبَنِيهِ يَوْمَئِذٍ كما هو اليوم. (101) When the Trumpet is blown (for the establishment of the Hour), there will be no ties of kinship between them that benefit them through mutual affection (التعاطف) and mercy (التراحم), or that they boast of. And that is due to the extremity of confusion (الحيرة) and the dominance of astonishment (الدّهشة), such that: **يَفِرُّ الْمَرْءُ مِنْ أَخِيهِ وَأُمِّهِ وَأَبِيهِ وَصاحِبَتِهِ وَبَنِيهِ يَوْمَئِذٍ ** (on the day when a man will flee from his brother, 34. and his mother and his father, 35. and his spouse and his sons.) just as it is today.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: "Every lineage (حسب) and kinship (نسب) will be severed, except for my lineage (حسب) and my kinship (نسب)."

وَلا يَتَساءَلُونَ ** (nor will they question one another) and none of them will question another due to being preoccupied with his own self. And this does not contradict His Almighty saying: وَأَقْبَلَ بَعْضُهُمْ عَلى بَعْضٍ يَتَساءَلُونَ ** (And some of them will turn to others, questioning one another) because this (the first state) is at the time of the blowing (of the Trumpet), and that (the second state) is at the time of the reckoning (المحاسبة).

And in al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام regarding this verse, he said: "No one will advance on the Day of Resurrection except through deeds (الأعمال)."

And in al-Manaqib, narrated from Imam al-Sajjad عليه السلام regarding it: "By Allah, nothing will benefit you tomorrow except a provision (مقدّمة) that you send forth of righteous deed (عمل صالح)."

** (١٠٢) فَمَنْ ثَقُلَتْ مَوازِينُهُ [The things weighed are] his beliefs (عقائده) and his deeds (اعماله). Al-Qummi said [it refers] to good deeds (الأعمال الحسنة). فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ.**

(102) So those whose scales are heavy—it is they who are the felicitous.

(١٠٣) وَمَنْ خَفَّتْ مَوازِينُهُ (103) But those whose scales are light He said: [It is] from those good deeds. I say: The verification of the meaning of the weight (الوزن) has already passed in Surat al-A'raf. فَأُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ خَسِرُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ (It is they who have ruined their souls) They cheated them (their souls) by wasting the time of their perfection (استكمالها) and nullifying their readiness to attain their completeness (كمالها). فِي جَهَنَّمَ خالِدُونَ (in hell [to remain] immortal.)

(١٠٤) تَلْفَحُ وُجُوهَهُمُ النَّارُ ** (104) The Fire will scorch their faces It burns them. Al-Qummi said: It flares up against them and burns them. It was said: Al-lafḥ (اللّفح) is like al-nafkh (النفخ), except that it is stronger in effect than al-nafkh (النفخ). وَهُمْ فِيها كالِحُونَ ** while they are grimacing therein. due to the severity of the burning. And al-kulūḥ (الكلوح) is the shrinking of the lips from the teeth. Al-Qummi: meaning, with open mouths, with faces covered in dust (متربّدي الوجوه).

(١٠٦) أَلَمْ تَكُنْ آياتِي تُتْلى عَلَيْكُمْ فَكُنْتُمْ بِها تُكَذِّبُونَ (106) Were My signs not recited to you, and did you use to deny them? That is, that will be said to them as a rebuke (تأنيباً) and a reminder (تذكيراً).

(١٠٧) قالُوا رَبَّنا غَلَبَتْ عَلَيْنا شِقْوَتُنا (107) They will say: 'Our Lord, our wretchedness overcame us, It dominated us such that our conditions led to an evil outcome. And it was recited شقاوتنا (our wretchedness) with the alif and the fathah on the shīn. In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: They became wretched through their deeds. وَكُنَّا قَوْماً ضالِّينَ ** and we were a straying people. straying from the truth. (١٠٧) رَبَّنا أَخْرِجْنا مِنْها ** Our Lord, bring us out of it. from the Fire. فَإِنْ عُدْنا الى التكذيب Then if we return to denial, **فَإِنَّا ظالِمُونَ ** we will indeed be wrongdoers.' to ourselves.

(١٠٨) قالَ اخْسَؤُا فِيها (108) He will say, 'Be gone into it! Be silent, a silence of humiliation, for it is not a station for supplication. [The word اخْسَؤُا is derived] from the driving away (خسئات) of the dog when you rebuke it and it is driven away. وَلا تُكَلِّمُونِ and do not speak to Me!' Al-Qummi: It reached me—and Allah knows best—that they crushed one another for seventy years until they reached the bottom (قعر) of Hell.

** (١٠٩) إِنَّهُ كانَ فَرِيقٌ مِنْ عِبادِي يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنا آمَنَّا فَاغْفِرْ لَنا وَارْحَمْنا وَأَنْتَ خَيْرُ الرَّاحِمِينَ** (109) Indeed there was a group of My servants who would say, 'Our Lord! We believe, so forgive us and have mercy on us, and You are the best of the merciful.'

(١١٠) فَاتَّخَذْتُمُوهُمْ سِخْرِيًّا mockery (هزواً). It is read with a ḍammah on the sīn (بضم السين). حَتَّى أَنْسَوْكُمْ ذِكْرِي ** (until they made you forget My remembrance) due to your excessive preoccupation with mocking them (بالاستهزاء بهم), so you did not fear Me concerning My friends (أوليائي). وَكُنْتُمْ مِنْهُمْ تَضْحَكُونَ ** (and you used to laugh at them.) mocking them (استهزاء بهم).

(١١١) إِنِّي جَزَيْتُهُمُ الْيَوْمَ بِما صَبَرُوا على أذاكم أَنَّهُمْ هُمُ الْفائِزُونَ ** (111) Indeed, I have rewarded them today for their patience against your persecution, that they are the triumphant ones. They are singled out for triumph (الفوز) with their desired outcomes. And it was read with the kasrah of the hamzah (بكسر الهمزة).

(112) He said: Allah the Almighty said, or the angel commanded to question them. And 'Say' (قل) was recited as a command to the angel: **كَمْ لَبِثْتُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (How long did you remain in the earth) alive and dead in the graves, (as) a number of years.

(١١٣) قالُوا لَبِثْنا يَوْماً أَوْ بَعْضَ يَوْمٍ (113) They will say, 'We stayed for a day or part of a day.' [This is] an expression of deeming the duration of their stay therein short. فَسْئَلِ الْعادِّينَ 'So ask those who keep count.' Al-Qummi said: Ask the angels who count the days for us and record our hours and the deeds that we earned therein.
(114) He said, and he recited: Say: إِنْ لَبِثْتُمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً لَوْ أَنَّكُمْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (You did not tarry but a little, had you known.)

(١١٥) أَفَحَسِبْتُمْ أَنَّما خَلَقْناكُمْ عَبَثاً (115) Did you suppose that We created you aimlessly,

This is a reprimand (توبيخ) to them for their heedlessness (تغافلهم). Meaning: We did not create you for Our amusement (تلهياً بكم); rather, We created you so that We might charge you with worship (لنتعبّدكم) and so that We might recompense you for your deeds (أعمالكم).

And this is like a proof (كالدليل) for the Resurrection (البعث): وَأَنَّكُمْ إِلَيْنا لا تُرْجَعُونَ and that you will not be brought back to Us?

It was recited (وقرء) with the opening of the tā (بفتح التاء) and the breaking of the jīm (وكسر الجيم) [i.e., تَرْجِعُونَ].

In al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, did not create His creation aimlessly (عبثاً) and did not leave them neglected (سُدى). Rather, He created them to manifest His power (لإِظهار قدرته) and to charge them with His obedience (ليكلّفهم على طاعته) so that they may thereby merit His pleasure (رضوانه). He did not create them to attract benefit (منفعة) from them, nor to repel harm (مضرّة) through them. Rather, He created them to benefit them and to deliver them to His bliss (نعيمه).

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): It was said to Him: "Were we created for annihilation (للفناء)?" He said: "Hush (مَهْ)! We were created for permanence (للبقاء). How could it be otherwise, when His Paradise does not perish (لا تبيد) and His Fire does not subside (لا تخمد)? Rather, we are merely transferred from one abode (دار) to another abode (دار)."

(١١٦) فَتَعالَى اللهُ الْمَلِكُ الْحَقُ Who is rightful to possess the Sovereignty (الملك). لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْكَرِيمِ. (There is no god except Him, the Lord of the noble Throne.)

(١١٧) وَمَنْ يَدْعُ مَعَ اللهِ إِلهاً آخَرَ لا بُرْهانَ لَهُ بِهِ (117) And whoever invokes another god besides Allah, for which he has no proof— for falsehood has no proof for it. By that, He drew attention to the fact that adopting a religion (التدين) for which there is no evidence is prohibited, let alone that for which the evidence indicates the contrary. فَإِنَّما حِسابُهُ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِ ** his reckoning is only with his Lord. so He will recompense him according to the measure of what he deserves. إِنَّهُ لا يُفْلِحُ الْكافِرُونَ Indeed, the faithless will not be felicitous. The Surah began by affirming the felicity (فلاح) of the believers and concluded it by negating felicity (الفلاح) from the faithless.

(١١٨) وَقُلْ رَبِّ اغْفِرْ وَارْحَمْ وَأَنْتَ خَيْرُ الرَّاحِمِينَ. (118) And say, 'My Lord! Forgive and have mercy, and You are the best of the merciful.'

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), who said: "Whoever recites Surah al-Mu'minun, Allah seals for him with bliss (السعادة). And if he persists in reciting it every Friday, his abode will be in the Highest Paradise (الفردوس الأعلى) with the Prophets (النبيّين) and the Messengers (المرسلين)." O Allah, grant us the provision of its recitation, by the right of Muhammad and his Household (may the blessings of Allah be upon him and his Household).
